claim: Use a vector database like Redis for semantic search and metadata filtering to ground agents in relevant, authoritative knowledge sources
actionable: Store chunked documentation in Redis as vectors with metadata, then use semantic search at query time for fast, filtered retrieval
source_title: Building SRE Agents with Redis Context Engine

claim: Implement chunking strategies that preserve document integrity (e.g., keep CLI/API docs whole, set chunk limits for long documents) to prevent knowledge fragmentation
actionable: Define different chunking rules per document type—keep command references intact, limit maximum chunks per document to prevent dominance
source_title: Building SRE Agents with Redis Context Engine

claim: Separate agents into specialized roles (knowledge agent, chat agent, triage agent) with a router to classify incoming requests and dispatch appropriately
actionable: Design a query router that classifies user requests and directs them to the right specialized agent; limit each agent's scope and tool access
source_title: Building SRE Agents with Redis Context Engine

claim: Use smaller, faster models (mini/nano LLMs) for classification, knowledge retrieval, and parallel per-topic research to reduce latency and cost at scale
actionable: Route heavy reasoning to large models but use smaller models for routing, summarization, and parallel topic extraction
source_title: Building SRE Agents with Redis Context Engine

claim: Implement semantic caching to avoid redundant LLM calls when the same query (or semantically similar queries) has been answered before
actionable: Embed incoming queries, search a vector cache of previous queries, and return cached results if similarity exceeds a threshold
source_title: Building SRE Agents with Redis Context Engine

claim: Models experience degradation in performance well before context window hits capacity—loss-in-middle and recency bias typically appear at ~50% capacity
actionable: Monitor context window usage at 50% threshold, compress large tool outputs to ~500 tokens, and use expand-context tools for detailed info on demand
source_title: Building SRE Agents with Redis Context Engine

claim: Allow users to add custom operational runbooks (skills) and pinned policies to influence agent behavior without making agents black boxes
actionable: Enable customers to ingest markdown runbooks as first-class documents and pin critical policies to always be included in system context
source_title: Building SRE Agents with Redis Context Engine

claim: Use hybrid search (combining vector similarity with full-text search using RRF fusion) to handle both semantic and exact-match queries
actionable: Combine vector similarity and full-text search for incident tickets; use retrieval rank fusion to blend both signals
source_title: Building SRE Agents with Redis Context Engine

claim: Implement a fact corrector pass before recommendations reach users to remove unsafe or fabricated commands using knowledge base access
actionable: Run a second model pass over final outputs to verify command safety against knowledge base before returning to user
source_title: Building SRE Agents with Redis Context Engine

claim: Extract and store agent memory in two layers: short-term (session messages and tool calls) and long-term (distilled ephemeral memories with topics and entities)
actionable: Store session data in-memory, run regular extraction pipelines to graduate important facts to long-term memory with entity and topic extraction
source_title: Building SRE Agents with Redis Context Engine

claim: Every agent recommendation should include citations showing which documents were accessed and which tools were called for verifiability
actionable: Require agents to include source citations (knowledge base references + tool calls) with all recommendations
source_title: Building SRE Agents with Redis Context Engine

claim: Use agents on scheduled intervals (cron jobs) for proactive monitoring and health diagnostics rather than only reactive query responses
actionable: Set up scheduled agent runs to monitor infrastructure and generate daily health summaries before issues arise
source_title: Building SRE Agents with Redis Context Engine
